pages.raw_text:
The theatrical performance given
yesterday at the assembly room of
the chamber of commerce by the
members of the elocution depart
ment of the Woman's club was a
great success and every person in
the cast handled her part in most
admirable manner.


Mrs. B. F. Jones was chairman of
the committee and the rehearsals
were held under the instruction OF
Miss M. Cronin.
The program was opened with a
reading from the second scene OF
Hamlet by Mrs. Kleber. Follow.
ing the first number, the three act
drama entitled "Rebekah's Tramp
with cast of IA characters, was
presented.


Mrs. Rokeman carried the. heavy
leading part and was exceptionally
clever. while Mrs. G. Du Vail Brown
as the proud, naughty heiress, was
heard to advantage.
Mrs. w. r. McRoberts as Mrs.
De Lame, mother of charity; Miss
Silver as "Rebekah" and Miss Low
erick as "Clarissa Goldman" as the
heroine and the maid who was pin
ing for a husband, were very good
and handled their parts in man
ner that added much to the interest
of hte play. e
Mrs. S. E. Peterson as Katie
Cammer," the Irish servant, and
Mrs. D. R. Wallace as "Gyt," the
negro servant, carried the comedy
parts of the acts and brought forth
round after round of applause. Mrs.
A. M. Riley as Meg" the woman
supposed to be crazy. came in for a
good share of applause and appre-
citation.


The remaining members Of the
cast included seven girls. friends of
Rebekah. These parts were handled
by Miss Hazel DIN. Miss Sicily.
Miss Spiger. Miss Morgan, Miss
See. Mrs. Mckinley and Mrs. Baker!
About 300 people witnessed the
performance.
